enonce.texte: You will listen to a short documentary about sustainable practices in an African community. Listen carefully and answer the questions that follow.
images: A radio or podcast icon with sound waves, representing listening comprehension

questions.texte: According to the speaker, what are the main environmental challenges facing the community?
questions.reponse: The main environmental challenges are waste management and the depletion of natural resources.

questions.texte: List two sustainable practices mentioned in the documentary.
questions.reponse: Recycling waste and using renewable energy sources.

questions.texte: Analyze the speaker's argument for why the community should adopt renewable energy. What is the primary reason given?
questions.reponse: The speaker argues that adopting renewable energy is crucial because it is a sustainable and clean alternative to fossil fuels, which will help preserve the environment for future generations and reduce pollution.

questions.texte: The speaker says, "We should have our waste collected regularly." What is the meaning of the grammatical structure "have our waste collected"?
questions.reponse: It is the causative form, meaning to arrange for someone else to do the service of collecting the waste.

enonce.texte: Match the words from the lesson with their correct definitions.

questions.reponse: To process used materials into new products to prevent waste.
An energy source that is not depleted when used, such as solar or wind power.
A practice that can be maintained over a long period without harming the environment.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using the causative form (have/get + object + past participle).

questions.texte: Someone collects our household waste every Monday.
questions.reponse: We have our household waste collected every Monday.

questions.texte: The villagers will ask a technician to install the solar panels.
questions.reponse: The villagers will have the solar panels installed by a technician.

enonce.texte:
Read the scenario below and answer the questions.

Scenario: Your local community is facing a waste management problem. The council is considering two options: (1) have a new incinerator built to burn the waste, or (2) implement a comprehensive recycling program.
images: A split image showing a waste incinerator on one side and a recycling center with bins on the other

questions.texte: Evaluate the two options presented in the scenario. Which option is more sustainable, and why?
questions.reponse: The recycling program is more sustainable. Burning waste in an incinerator releases harmful pollutants and does not conserve resources. Recycling reduces the need for new raw materials and saves energy.

questions.texte: Propose how you could persuade local leaders to adopt the more sustainable solution. What arguments would you use?
questions.reponse: I would argue that a recycling program creates jobs, reduces pollution, and conserves resources, ensuring a cleaner and healthier environment for future generations. I would present data on the long-term costs of an incinerator versus the benefits of recycling, emphasizing the community's health and well-being.

enonce.texte: Listen to the short talk about the community's conservation efforts. Fill in the blanks in the summary using the correct form of the causative (have/get + object + past participle).
images: A group of community members working together to clean up a local park

questions.texte: The council decided to ______ the old, inefficient appliances ______. (replace)
To promote recycling, the community will ______ a new educational program ______. (develop)
They are planning to ______ the public lighting system ______ to use solar power. (modify)
